description:
`bl plugin enable <name>` stores `config_file` as a path relative to .balls/plugins/, but `Plugin::resolve` (src/plugin/runner.rs:25) joins `config_file` against the repo ROOT. The two disagree on the base, so the --config path handed to the plugin subprocess does not exist.

Repro (verified empirically, debug build of main 87fdd38):
  bl plugin enable github      # stores config_file:"github.json", writes file to .balls/plugins/github.json
  # task event dispatch invokes:  balls-plugin-github push --config <ROOT>/github.json --auth-dir <ROOT>/.balls/local/plugins/github
  # but the file is at <ROOT>/.balls/plugins/github.json  -> --config points at a nonexistent path

enable() default is format!("{name}.json") (src/plugin_admin.rs:81) and it writes the file via effective_plugins_dir().join(config_file) = <root>/.balls/plugins/<name>.json (src/plugin_admin.rs:25). An explicit --config-file VALUE is broken the same way. There is NO input to `bl plugin enable` that yields a runtime-resolvable entry: the only form Plugin::resolve accepts is a root-relative `.balls/plugins/<name>.json`, and passing that makes enable double the prefix.

Severity: (a) every `bl plugin enable` produces a non-functional plugin; (b) re-running `bl plugin enable github` on a correctly hand-configured repo OVERWRITES a working `config_file` (e.g. the live repo's `.balls/plugins/github.json`) back to the broken `github.json`. The dogfooded balls repo works today only because its .balls/config.json was hand-written with the root-relative form.

Test gap: tests/plugin_admin.rs::enable_standalone_inserts_entry_and_creates_file asserts config_file=="github.json" and that the FILE exists, but never asserts the runtime can resolve it -- the bug is encoded as expected behaviour, suite stays green.

Pre-existing in bl-32e5 (predates bl-8a9a); surfaced by the bl-b0b9 audit of the interim plugin-config split. Fix: pick one base. Either Plugin::resolve joins config_file against effective_plugins_dir(), or enable stores+writes a root-relative path -- one of the two, consistently. NOTE bl-e609 is in progress and rewrites plugin_admin to write into project.json; coordinate so the fix is not lost.
title: bl plugin enable writes a config_file the plugin runtime cannot resolve
